info.title: CRM API
info.description:
### Basics
- Use HTTPS in all requests.
- You must set the Content-type HTTP header. We won't interpret your POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE request body without it.

### Rate limits
Limits are applied per client IP. Exceeding a limit returns HTTP `429 Too Many Requests` until the window resets.

| Surface | Limit |
| --- | --- |
| Default (all endpoints) | 500 requests / minute |
| `POST /auth/token` | 20 requests / minute |
| `/auth/twofa/*` (group total) | 30 requests / 5 minutes |
| `/public-tool/*` (group total) | 30 requests / 5 minutes |

The stricter caps stack on top of the default — a request to `/auth/token` counts against both the 20/minute auth limit and the 500/minute global limit.

### Authentication
Authenticate your API requests by providing a bearer token, which identifies a single CRM user. We prefer tokens to be sent in the Authorization HTTP header of your requests. However, you may also pass tokens in all API calls as a parameter called token.

When a request is made with an expired token, it is automatically reauthenticated and a refreshed token is returned in the `Authorization` response header. Use the new token for subsequent requests.

For Example:

```
GET /account HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Authorization: Bearer eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIU===
```

```
GET /account?token=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIU=== HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
```

### Response
Successful API call will return HTTP status code 200 along with JSON data.

All other HTTP status codes should be treated as fail.
With every failed API call there will be returned HTTP status code, error message and for
some API calls in depth error description.

### Pagination
For methods that are able to return large data lists, there will be used pagination.
All meta data about pages will be appended to successful response.

For example:
```json
{
    "data": [
        ...
    ],
    "meta": {
        "pagination": {
            "total": 1,
            "count": 1,
            "perPage": 100,
            "currentPage": 1,
            "totalPages": 1,
            "links": []
        }
    }
}
```

### Distributor switch
To switch distributor set the `App-Account-Distributor` HTTP header. Only valid values will be processed.

### Return different lang translations
To change return language set the `App-Response-Language` HTTP header. Only valid values will be processed.

post.description: Batching allows you to call several endpoints in a single HTTP request. Each batched request response is equivalent to the response you could expect from each endpoint if performed as separate requests. Only specific endpoints can be used in batch request (see the endpoint description)

requests.description: Object key is used as unique identifier in response

method.description: Allowed values: GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE

params.description: Used only for: POST, PATCH, PUT methods

200.description: Batch response with results for each request

data.description: Object with keys matching request identifiers

distributors.description: Array of distributor ids that have access the firmware. Only shown if accessScope = distributor

bearerAuth.description:
JWT Bearer token authentication.

**How to obtain a token:**
1. Call `POST /auth/token` with your credentials
2. Use the returned token in the `Authorization` header

**Example:**
```
Authorization: Bearer eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIU...
```

**Token refresh:**
Before a token expires, a new token is sent in the response header. Use the new token for subsequent requests.
